claim: Speaker diarization (who speaks when) is as important as transcription for understanding conversations; it enables auto-dubbing, podcast tracking, and detecting interruptions
actionable: For any multi-speaker conversation analysis, always combine ASR with speaker diarization; don't treat transcription alone as sufficient for understanding
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI

claim: Open ASR leaderboards like Hugging Face show 11.4% WER on Nvidia Parakeet, but real-world multi-speaker distant-microphone audio gets 26% WER on the same model - benchmarks don't reflect production conditions
actionable: When evaluating speech models, test on YOUR actual audio (meeting room, multiple speakers, distance mics); don't assume leaderboard numbers apply to your use case
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI

claim: Reconciling ASR timestamps with diarization outputs is non-trivial because STT doesn't handle overlapping speech well and timestamps often disagree between the two systems
actionable: When combining ASR + diarization, implement a reconciliation step that handles word-level timestamp alignment and overlapping speech; don't just naive merge timestamps
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI

claim: Diarization error rate (DER) includes confusion (wrong speaker), false alarms (detecting speech when absent), and misdetection (missing speech); state-of-the-art is 2% on clean phone calls, 41% in noisy environments
actionable: Use DER as your metric for speaker diarization; understand that performance degrades dramatically in noisy/crowded settings; target <10% DER for production systems
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI

claim: Voice stress, prosody, and disfluency detection (how someone said something) can convey emotional state and emphasis that changes meaning - models should include these features in transcription
actionable: Extend voice AI beyond words: capture stress patterns, pause duration, and tone in transcripts; this helps downstream LLMs understand intent and emotion correctly
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI

claim: PyAnnote's exclusive diarization handles overlapping speech by selecting the most likely speaker before passing to STT, simplifying the reconciliation problem
actionable: Use PyAnnote's exclusive diarization option for overlapping speech; it pre-processes ambiguous regions to avoid ASR confusion and simplifies downstream processing
source_title: Beyond Transcription: Building Voice AI That Understands Conversations — Hervé Bredin, pyannoteAI
